included: assignment or copy of existing token handles
excluded: looking up existing text in the token registry
stringification or copying token text out of the implementation

model.Token: First-class target-native value abstraction for already-created token data.
model.TokenHandle: Opaque conformance-harness reference that may wrap a target-native Token value.
model.TokenIdentityDomain: Implementation-defined domain in which Token values have canonical equality/hash identity. The performance contract applies after tokens have been created in a compatible identity domain.
model.constant_time: Operation cost should be flat with respect to token text length once handles exist.
model.ratio_gate: Compare long-token and short-token timings for the same operation. The long-token timing should not grow materially with text length.

equal.expected_complexity: amortized O(1) with respect to token text length

hash.expected_complexity: amortized O(1) with respect to token text length

assign.input: One existing TokenHandle value assigned or copied to another handle variable
assign.expected_complexity: O(1) with respect to token text length

measurement.secondary_signal: absolute ns_per_op ceiling to catch pathological adapter or implementation behavior
measurement.note: Ratio gates are more portable than absolute timings across languages and machines.
